mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.09% (near zero) signals an extended monetary easing regime. Sustained low rates compress long-term discount rates and reduce term premiums, causing 10-year Treasury yields to decline as investors seek duration. This always-on mechanism operates whenever FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%.
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with near-zero rates
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 15 basis points over 20 trading days in more than 40% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 7.3% signals labor market slack. When UNRATE exceeds 7.0%, it indicates persistent economic weakness that depresses growth expectations, causing investors to rotate into safe-haven Treasuries and driving 10-year yields lower as risk appetite declines.
regime_hint: post-crisis labor market recovery with elevated slack
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield increases by more than 10 basis points over 20 trading days in more than 50% of triggered instances, or signal fails to predict negative equity returns in > 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) at 425,881 million dollars combined with housing starts (HOUST) at 891 thousand units indicate moderate demand. When both series show month-over-month growth below 1%, it signals weakening consumer demand and housing activity, reducing inflation expectations and causing 10-year yields to fall as growth concerns dominate.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM growth < 1% AND HOUST MoM growth < 1%
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in more than 45% of jointly-triggered instances, or interaction effect (joint signal) produces lower Rank IC than either series alone over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 99.98 (near 2017=100 baseline) signals stalled supply-side growth. When INDPRO declines month-over-month or remains flat for 2+ consecutive months, it indicates manufacturing weakness and reduced inflation pressure, causing 10-year Treasury yields to compress as stagflation fears recede.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO shows 2 consecutive months of MoM decline or zero growth
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield increases by more than 20 basis points over 60 trading days in more than 45% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) at 136,554 thousand persons combined with unemployment rate (UNRATE) at 7.3% creates a divergence signal. When PAYEMS growth decelerates below 100k/month while UNRATE remains elevated, it signals labor market momentum loss, triggering flight-to-safety Treasury demand and yield compression.
regime_hint: labor market deceleration in late-cycle environment
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in more than 50% of jointly-triggered instances, or the interaction signal fails to outperform either component series in directional accuracy over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) at 233.947 (index 1982-1984=100) combined with near-zero federal funds rate (0.09%) creates a real rate compression signal. When CPI growth accelerates above 2.5% YoY while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, the Fed is in effective easing mode, reducing real discount rates and causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise as inflation expectations increase.
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls in more than 55% of jointly-triggered instances, or the interaction effect produces negative Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) at 15,790.1 billion chained 2017 dollars represents late-cycle expansion. When quarterly GDP growth decelerates below 2.0% annualized while RSAFS shows weakness, it signals demand exhaustion and recession risk, causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall sharply as investors price in lower future growth and Fed rate cuts.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth < 2.0% AND RSAFS MoM growth < 0.5%
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with demand deceleration and recession risk
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 25 basis points over 60 trading days in more than 40% of jointly-triggered instances, or the interaction signal fails to predict negative equity returns in > 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 99.98 combined with payroll employment (PAYEMS) at 136,554K creates a supply-labor divergence. When INDPRO declines while PAYEMS growth remains positive, it signals productivity deterioration and cost-push inflation, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise as stagflation concerns increase and real returns compress.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM decline AND PAYEMS MoM growth > 50k
regime_hint: stagflation risk with productivity decline and employment resilience
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls in more than 50% of jointly-triggered instances, or the interaction signal produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.
